Package com.bfo.json

Class JsonWriter

java.lang.Object
com.bfo.json.JsonWriter
All Implemented Interfaces:
JsonStream
public class JsonWriter extends Object implements JsonStream
A JSON writer

  • Constructor Details

    • JsonWriter

      public JsonWriter()

  • Method Details

    • setSorted

      public JsonWriter setSorted(boolean sorted)
      Request that map keys are sorted before writing.
      Parameters:
      sorted - true if keys should be sorted
      Returns:
      this

    • isSorted

      public boolean isSorted()
      Description copied from interface: JsonStream
      Return true if this JsonStream would prefer Map keys to be sorted. Only event sources where the data is in memory (eg the Json object are expected to be able to comply with this. The default method implementation returns false.
      Specified by:
      isSorted in interface JsonStream

    • setCborDiag

      public JsonWriter setCborDiag(String option)
      Configure this JsonWriter to write CBOR-diag format. The specified value controls how buffer values will be serialised, either has hex or one of two variations of base64.
      Parameters:
      option - either "hex", "base64", "base64-standard", or null for normal Json
      Returns:
      this

    • setFloatFormat

      public JsonWriter setFloatFormat(String format)
      The String format to use when formatting a float. The default is "%.8g" Note that superfluous trailing zeros will trimmed from any formatted value.
      Parameters:
      format - the format, which will be passed to DecimalFormat
      Returns:
      this

    • setDoubleFormat

      public JsonWriter setDoubleFormat(String format)
      The String format to use when formatting a double. The default is "%.16g". Note that superfluous trailing zeros will trimmed from any formatted value.
      Parameters:
      format - the format, which will be passed to DecimalFormat
      Returns:
      this

    • setAllowNaN

      public JsonWriter setAllowNaN(boolean allow)
      Whether to allow NaN and Infinite values in the output. Both NaN and infinite values are disallowed in RFC8259. With this flag set, Infinite or NaN values are serialized as null, which matches web-browser behaviour. With this flag not set, an IOException is thrown during serialization
      Parameters:
      nan - the flag
      Returns:
      this

    • setIndent

      public JsonWriter setIndent(int size)
      Set whether the output is pretty-printed with newlines and indenting. A value of zero (the default) means no pretty-printing
      Parameters:
      size - the number of spaces to indent, or 0 for no pretty-printing
      Returns:
      this

    • setSpaceAfterColon

      public JsonWriter setSpaceAfterColon(boolean space)
      Set whether to add a space after colons when printing
      Parameters:
      space - the flag
      Returns:
      this

    • setSpaceAfterComma

      public JsonWriter setSpaceAfterComma(boolean space)
      Set whether to add a space after commas when printing. Ignored for pretty-printing, as commas are followed by newlines.
      Parameters:
      space - the flag
      Returns:
      this

    • setMaxArraySize

      public JsonWriter setMaxArraySize(int size)
      Set the maximum number of items to print in an array. Additional entries will be replaced with an ellipsis; the result will not be valid Json, but is useful for use in toString()
      Parameters:
      size - the maximum number of items to print, or 0 for no limit (the default)
      Returns:
      this

    • setMaxStringLength

      public JsonWriter setMaxStringLength(int size)
      Set the maximum length of a string. Additional characters will be replaced with an ellipsis
      Parameters:
      size - the maximum length of a string to print, or 0 for no limit (the default)
      Returns:
      this

    • setOutput

      public JsonWriter setOutput(Appendable out)
      Set the Appendable to write to
      Parameters:
      out - the output
      Returns:
      this

    • setOutput

      public JsonWriter setOutput(OutputStream out)
      Set the OutputStream to write to, encoding the text as UTF-8. This is equivalent to setOutput(new OutputStremWriter(out, "UTF-8")) except that it is unbuffered and faster.
      Parameters:
      out - the output
      Returns:
      this
      Since:
      2.1

    • setOutput

      public JsonWriter setOutput(ByteBuffer buf)
      Set the ByteBuffer to write to, encoding the text as UTF-8.
      Parameters:
      out - the ByteBuffer
      Returns:
      this
      Since:
      2.1

    • setOutput

      public JsonWriter setOutput(CharBuffer buf)
      Set the CharBuffer to write to
      Parameters:
      out - the output
      Returns:
      this

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Description copied from interface: JsonStream
      Reset the stream. When a single writer is reused for multiple object written in sequence, this can be called between each object to reset the internal state.
      Specified by:
      reset in interface JsonStream

    • event

      public boolean event(JsonStream.Event event) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: JsonStream
      Process an event. Return true if the stream has completed. Any events submitted after this will cause an error
      Specified by:
      event in interface JsonStream
      Returns:
      true if the stream has completed
      Throws:
      IOException
